---
title: "Upload Harmonized Data"
author: "World Bank / UNHCR Joint Team"
date: today
---

## Overview
Harmonized microdata and harmonization scripts are uploaded to **Datalibweb** via **PRIMUS**. After review, approved files are published to Datalibweb for 50by30 monitoring.

## Uploading via PRIMUS

Harmonized files are uploaded as a PRIMUS **transaction** using the `FDP-harmonized-data` process (`processid = 40`) by users with uploader status. 

All harmonized files must pass validation checks before they can be uploaded to Datalibweb. These confirm that the harmonized file conforms to the schema, and that mandatory variables are present and non-missing. Any deviation from the schema will trigger an error and prevent the harmonized file from being uploaded to Datalibweb. They check for missing values in mandatory variables, for negative or zero values in `welfare` and `weight`, that `welfare_self` is not greater than `welfare`, and that `hsize` is consistent with the number of unique `pid`s per `hhid`.

A harmonized data upload starts with a `.qml` file that returns a transaction ID. The `.qml` file contains metadata about the harmonized file, and calculates values for the 50by30 self-reliance indicator for the reviewer to approve. 

For convenience, validation checks, .qml creation and upload functions are combined in one script so that they can be run together. The script is available in the [`50by30-data` repository](https://github.com/worldbank/50by30-data) and below.

After uploading, the uploader confirms the draft transaction. Only then does it become visible to approvers.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Include the harmonization scripts
Upload the code used to harmonize the raw
data (e.g. the `.do` or `.R` files) together with the harmonized dataset, to the
**`Programs/`** folder on Datalibweb. Add them as additional files on the same
transaction ID so the harmonization is fully reproducible from source.
:::

::: {.panel-tabset}

## R

```r
library(primus)

```

## Stata

```stata


```

:::

## Reviewing & approving via PRIMUS

Once the uploader confirms the transaction to the harmonized collection, it is queued for review by users with approver status. Reviewers can approve or reject the transaction. 

Approved harmonized files are published to Datalibweb and used to monitor the 50by30 self-reliance indicator. Rejected files are returned to the uploader for correction and resubmission.

::: {.callout-warning}
## Note
A transaction cannot be approved or rejected more than once, and the action cannot be changed once it goes through.
:::

::: {.panel-tabset}

## R

```r
library(primus)

# Check pending transactions for review
primus_list_transactions(process_name = "RAWPROC", status = "pending")

# download the files for review
primus_download(
  process_name   = "RAWPROC",
  survey_id      = "AGO_2029_HBS_V01_M",
  transaction_id = "006-000327173-MNARAW-AGO-12944",
  dest_dir       = "c:/Temp/AGO_2029_HBS_V01_M"
)

# Approve a transaction
primus_action(
  process_name   = "RAWPROC",
  survey_id      = "AGO_2029_HBS_V01_M",
  transaction_id = "006-000327173-MNARAW-AGO-12944",
  decision       = "approve",
  comments       = "raw data approved for harmonization"
)

```

## Stata

```stata

* Check pending transactions for review
primus list, proc(6) status(pending)

* download the files for review
primus download, proc(6) surveyid(AGO_2029_HBS_V01_M) tranxid(006-000327173-MNARAW-AGO-12944) ///
    destdir(c:\Temp\AGO_2029_HBS_V01_M)

* Approve a transaction
primus action, tranxid(006-000327173-MNARAW-AGO-12944) proc(6) ///
    decision(approve) comments("raw data approved for harmonization")

```

:::
